rationale: The triggering process (A0) uses a Right-to-Left Override (RTLO/U+202E) character in its filename — 'cod.3aka3.scr' visually renders as 'rcs.3aka3.doc', a classic filename-spoofing technique used to disguise executables as documents. The file resides in C:\ProgramData\victim\ and was launched by explorer.exe (user double-clicked it), indicating social engineering delivery. Within seconds, the same process initiated an outbound TCP connection to 192.168.0.5:1234 (EV-001), a non-standard port strongly suggestive of C2 communication. The process then spawned cmd.exe (EV-002), which in turn spawned powershell.exe (EV-003), forming a classic malware execution chain (SCR → CMD → PowerShell) from a suspicious working directory — no legitimate screensaver or document would exhibit this behavior.
